name: Entre Dúas Rías Trail
description: This difficult 5.6-mile (9.0 km) jogging trail near Ares and Mugardos offers coastal views, forest paths, and 909 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Jogging around Mugardos offers a diverse range of routes within this fishing borough nestled in the Ferrol estuary. The region features varied landscapes, including a rugged coastline with dramatic cliffs and beaches, alongside a mix of inland terrain. Paths often traverse the Galician coast, providing opportunities to run through natural beauty and local culture. The area's geography supports a variety of running experiences, from coastal paths to more challenging routes with elevation changes. Best jogging routes around Mugardos The most popular jogging route is Entre Dúas Rías Trail, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 13 minutes to complete, featuring varied terrain and coastal views. Another top favourite among local runners is Tidal Mill – Punta de Illa loop from O Igrexario, a moderate 7.8 miles (12.5 km) path that winds along the coastline, offering views of the estuary. Local runners also love the Punta Coitelada – View of O Segaño Beach loop from Mugardos, a 15.4 miles (24.8 km) trail leading through rugged coastline, often completed in about 3 hours 12 minutes. Jogging around Mugardos is defined by its rugged coastline, diverse landscapes, and charming fishing villages. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Mugardos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore Mugardos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Mugardos?
A: There are over 65 running routes available in Mugardos, catering to various fitness levels and preferences. These include a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Mugardos?
A: The running trails in Mugardos offer a diverse range of terrain. You can expect paths that traverse the rugged Galician coastline, providing breathtaking views of the Atlantic, as well as inland routes through varied landscapes. Many paths feature paved surfaces, and you'll find routes with varying degrees of elevation gain, from minimal climbs to significant ascents.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy run?
A: Yes, Mugardos offers routes suitable for beginners and those seeking an easy run. While many trails feature varied terrain and elevation, there are at least 3 easy routes available. These typically involve shorter loops with minimal elevation, perfect for a relaxed jog.

Q: What do other runners say about the routes in Mugardos?
A: The running routes in Mugardos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore the area's varied terrain, often praising the scenic coastal paths and the blend of natural beauty with local culture.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural features to see while jogging in Mugardos?
A: Absolutely! Many routes offer opportunities to see notable landmarks and natural features. For instance, you might encounter the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6599577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magnolia of Xuvia&lt;/a&gt;, recognized as a unique tree in Galicia. Other points of interest include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3546198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Martiño de Xuvia&lt;/a&gt; or the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5770278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontedeume Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which offers beautiful photo opportunities.

Q: Can I find running routes that offer coastal views?
A: Yes, coastal beauty is a significant draw in Mugardos. Many running paths traverse the rugged coastline, providing stunning views of the Atlantic and the Ferrol estuary. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31923174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tidal Mill – Punta de Illa loop from O Igrexario&lt;/a&gt; wind along the coastline, offering picturesque vistas.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Mugardos?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Mugardos are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31923174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tidal Mill – Punta de Illa loop from O Igrexario&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24660425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Coitelada – View of O Segaño Beach loop from Mugardos&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied distances and scenery.

Q: Are the running trails in Mugardos accessible by public transport?
A: Mugardos is a charming fishing borough within the Ferrol estuary. While specific public transport connections to every trailhead may vary, the town itself is generally accessible. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or regional transport options to plan your journey to the starting points of your chosen running routes.

Q: Is parking available near the running trails?
A: Given Mugardos's nature as a local community and a destination for outdoor activities, parking is generally available in and around the town and near popular trailheads. However, specific parking availability can depend on the exact starting point of your chosen route and the time of day. It's recommended to check local maps or komoot route details for parking suggestions.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: While the immediate area around Mugardos is known for its coastal beauty, the broader region does feature natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/985047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belelle River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. You might find routes that incorporate or are near such features, especially if you explore trails slightly further afield from the main town.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Mugardos?
A: Many outdoor trails in Galicia are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy a run with your canine companion. However, it's always best to check specific trail regulations or local signage, especially in protected natural areas or private lands, to ensure dogs are permitted and if they need to be on a leash.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Mugardos?
A: Mugardos, located in Galicia, offers a pleasant climate for running for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal. Summer can also be great, especially for coastal runs, though it might be warmer. Winter runs are possible, but be prepared for cooler, potentially wetter weather typical of the Galician coast.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Entre Dúas Rías Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain, characteristic of the Galician coast. While some sections might be family-friendly singletrack, others can be quite challenging with continuous ascents and descents, technical parts, and even 'roller coaster' sections. Expect dirt paths and potentially uneven surfaces, especially on routes with significant elevation changes. Good physical condition and sturdy shoes are recommended for the more demanding parts.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Joggers can enjoy diverse Galician landscapes. The route passes by significant landmarks such as the Castillo de La Palma and offers stunning vistas from the La Bailadora Viewpoint and Battery. You'll experience a mix of coastal views, potentially alongside beaches and cliffs, and verdant inland areas with riverbank woods.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and descriptions of challenging sections, the Entre Dúas Rías Trail is generally more suited for experienced joggers with good physical condition. However, the broader 'Entre Dúas Rías' network includes varied segments; this specific Komoot tour is considered difficult. Beginners should be prepared for significant elevation changes and technical terrain.

faqs.question: Is the Entre Dúas Rías Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, this specific Komoot tour, named 'Montefaro Circular of Bailadora', is designed as a circular route, meaning it's a loop trail.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Entre Dúas Rías Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact trailhead are not provided, trails in the Ares and Mugardos area of Galicia typically have designated parking areas near popular access points. It's advisable to check local maps or community forums for the most precise parking locations close to your intended starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Entre Dúas Rías Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, many outdoor trails in Galicia are dog-friendly, but leash laws and specific regulations can vary. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other trail users, and to carry waste bags.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The Galician coast experiences mild weather, making spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) ideal for jogging. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or heavy winter rains, allowing for comfortable exploration of both coastal and forested sections.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit to jog the Entre Dúas Rías Trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required for jogging on the Entre Dúas Rías Trail or similar public trails in the Galicia region of Spain.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with other trails. You will find sections that are also part of the Mtb Entre dúas Rías, Mtb Entre dos Rías, and Camiño do Espiño.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or facilities near the trail?
faqs.answer: The Entre Dúas Rías trails are located near the towns of Ares and Mugardos. These towns offer various amenities, including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options, where you can refuel or relax before or after your jog.

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring sturdy trail running shoes with good grip, sufficient water, snacks for energy, and appropriate clothing for the weather conditions. A phone for navigation and emergencies is also advisable.
